description_long: This object is a tall, ornate stone cross mounted on a rectangular stone base, surrounded by a patch of mossy, weathered ground. The cross features a circular ring intersecting its arms, characteristic of a Celtic cross, and is adorned with intricate, raised patterns and circular motifs along its surface. The base consists of several stone slabs, some of which are cracked or uneven, with visible moss and signs of age. The stone appears dark gray, with lighter patches indicating lichen or weathering, and the overall structure gives an impression of significant age and historical significance. The cross stands vertically and is robust, likely intended to serve as a protective or commemorative marker. Its substantial mass and solid construction suggest it is a fixed, immovable barrier in its environment.
description: A tall, intricately patterned stone Celtic cross on a weathered, mossy stone base, showing signs of age and historical character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the cross, detailed patterns, and the mossy, cracked stone base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the cross and the layered structure of the stone base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with visible weathering and the circular ring of the cross.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, highlighting the cross's profile and the uneven, mossy base.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, cemeteries, or as a boundary marker in parks or near old churches, often positioned adjacent to pathways or at the edge of open spaces.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright with its base resting on or slightly embedded in the earth or stone foundation.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface erosion, chipping along the edges, and discoloration from moss and lichen growth, especially on the base and lower sections. Weathering from rain and wind has softened some of the carved details, and the top surfaces show more pronounced fading and pitting.
attribute_historical_markings: Intricate raised patterns and circular motifs typical of Celtic crosses, covering the front and rear faces. No visible inscriptions or text.
